source_pr.title: executor: Use multiple goroutines to do join

symptoms: pass make race. | result = tk.MustQuery("select * from t left outer join t1 on t.c1 = t1.c1 and t.c1 != 1 order by t1.c1") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: pass make race. | result = tk.MustQuery("select * from t left outer join t1 on t.c1 = t1.c1 and t.c1 != 1 order by t1.c1") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The join test was flaky because it asserted on row order without an explicit ORDER BY. Join output order is not guaranteed, and multi-goroutine join execution can make the observed order vary across runs. This PR stabilizes it by adding an ORDER BY to the query in the test.
fix_pattern: Add ORDER BY (or sort results) before assertions to enforce deterministic row ordering in tests.

evidence: evidence: pass make race. | result = tk.MustQuery("select * from t left outer join t1 on t.c1 = t1.c1 and t.c1 != 1 order by t1.c1") (executor/executor_test.go)
